Our company delivers comprehensive energy and related services to commercial and industrial clients, as well as to several private corporations, acting as both an Independent Power Producer (IPP) and Captive Power Producer (CPP). We offer end-to-end Engineering, Procurement, Construction and Commissioning (EPCC) services for wind and wind-solar hybrid power projects, covering everything from site assessment and project development to operational management.

The leadership team conducts meetings with the customers and other value chain partners periodically.

The company is committed to leveraging the growing opportunities in the renewable energy sector to deliver Clean and Green Power, thereby contributing to societal well-being and environmental sustainability and supporting India’s national goal of Generating 500 GW of non-fossil fuel-based energy capacity by 2030.
Failure to comply with emission norms could lead to a negative/ inevitable long-term impact on the environment, society and may also lead to significant reputational damage.

The company has an effective monitoring mechanism in place for emission management, actively supporting India’s commitment to achieving net zero emissions by 2070. In the FY2025, KPEL’s renewable energy projects have resulted in an estimated carbon dioxide (CO₂) savings of 5,60,156 tons.

The company is committed to deliver high-quality wind energy and wind-solar hybrid energy infrastructure at competitive prices within committed timelines, while maintaining a customer-centric approach to build an ethically strong and stakeholder-friendly corporate entity.

Power generation operations present significant health and safety risks such as risk associated with operating heavy machinery, electrocution risk, etc. to employees and workers working at power plants. Further, violations of health and safety standards could result in monetary penalties and reputational damage.

The company has a Health, Safety and Environment (HSE) Policy and is committed to conduct all activities in a manner that eliminates hazards and minimizes risks to employees and assets, while fully complying with applicable health and safety regulations. It fosters a culture of continuous learning and strict adherence to HSE systems and procedures among all employees and contractors.

The process of project development entails various risks, ranging from obtaining building permits and acquiring suitable land to managing logistics and Right-of-Way (RoW) challenges. These uncertainties expose our business to potential project delays, cancellations, or write offs, which can significantly impact on our financial performance.

The Company is committed to diligently address project development risks and implement robust strategies to minimise their potential consequences by strengthening project planning and execution capabilities to ensure efficient and timely delivery by conducting comprehensive feasibility studies, optimising project timelines, and establishing effective project management processes.

With increasing awareness of ESG, environmental, social, and governance issues have become more important than ever to investors, customers, stakeholders, employees, communities, and regulators. Demonstrating strong ESG performance can enhance access to capital, attract top talent, and unlock new business opportunities.

The company adopts an innovative approach to maximize the efficiency of its wind assets by utilizing a state-of-the-art Centralized Monitoring System (CMS) and a dedicated Network Operations Centre (NOC). This system enables us to monitor, analyze and address any issues promptly, leading to increased asset efficiency, Predictive Maintenance for Optimal Performance, 24/7 operations, automated reporting for informed decision-making, continuous improvement through data insights.

Company’s revenue streams are closely tied to capital expenditures in the wind energy sector, primarily driven by Independent Power Producers (IPP) and Captive Power Producers (CPP). Therefore, fluctuations in the capital expenditure scenario and cycle, such as reduced investments by either IPPs or CPPs, have the potential to adversely affect our financial performance.
By strengthening customer relationships, building strong partnerships and delivering value-added services, we aim to foster customer loyalty and retain a stable base of customers.

KP Energy has set a strategic roadmap aligned with India’s renewable energy targets, aiming to commission 10 GW of renewable capacity at group level, supported by a strong project pipeline with over 2.26 GW across multiple sites. This robust pipeline ensures multi-year revenue visibility and supports steady growth in its core Engineering, Procurement, Construction, and Commissioning (EPCC) Business segment. Beyond onshore projects, KP Energy is also actively exploring 1–2 GW of offshore wind projects in Gujarat and Tamil Nadu and has distinguished itself as the first Company to advance wind resource deployment in South Gujarat. In the Independent Power Producer (IPP) Business segment the Company has set an ambitious target to expand its IPP capacity to 100 MW by the end of FY2027. Simultaneously, its Operations and Maintenance (O&M) business, is poised for continued expansion in line with project commissioning. These combined efforts reflect KP Energy’s integrated and forward-looking approach to driving sustained, long-term value and leadership in the renewable energy sector.

At K.P. Energy Limited, we remain committed to embedding sustainability into every aspect of our business, recognizing that our long-term success depends on balancing environmental stewardship, social responsibility, and governance excellence. While we continue to face challenges such as regulatory dynamics, climate-related risks and community integration in project areas, our focus is on expanding renewable energy capacity, reducing our carbon footprint, strengthening community engagement and enhancing compliance and transparency. During the year, we have successfully commissioned wind projects contributing to clean energy generation, advanced livelihood initiatives in local communities and reinforced governance systems through robust compliance practices. We view ESG not merely as a reporting requirement but as a driver of resilience, trust and long-term value creation for all stakeholders.

KPEL has a Board approved Anti-Corruption and Anti-Bribery Policy that forms a core part of its corporate governance framework. Rooted in the principles of ethical conduct, integrity and accountability, the Company’s governance philosophy drives responsible decision-making and reinforces its commitment to fair and transparent business practices across all levels of operation. KPEL maintains a zero-tolerance policy towards bribery, corruption, facilitation payments, kickbacks and any form of unethical or fraudulent behaviour, whether in dealings with government authorities or private entities. This is clearly outlined in the Company’s Anti-Corruption and Anti-Bribery Policy, which applies to all employees, directors, contractors, agents and third parties associated with the organization. Any offer, request, or suspicion of a bribe or improper payment must be strictly refused and immediately reported to the Vigilance and Ethics Officer, as per the procedures defined in the policy. The Company emphasizes regular awareness to ensure that all stakeholders understand their obligations under the policy. Compliance with these standards is periodically monitored and assessed to identify and mitigate any corruption-related risks.
Not applicable, since no instances of regulatory, judicial or law enforcement actions initiated or pending against the Company in relation to corruption or conflict of interest.

KPEL has well-established and approved code of conduct for all the board of directors and the senior management, available on KPEL’s website. The Code provides disclosure of any material, financial, or commercial transactions that may result in a conflict, prohibits the use of company property or information for personal gain and restricts acceptance of gifts or benefits that may influence decision-making. It also requires directors to exercise independent judgment and disclose any corporate opportunities they intend to pursue. Annual affirmations of compliance are obtained and any violations, including conflicts of interest, must be reported to the Chairperson of the Board or of the Audit Committee for appropriate investigation.

The Company endeavours to prevent all injuries and work-related illnesses and is committed to provide safe and healthy working environment. The Company provides first aid training to its employees at all sites and offices to ensure preparedness in handling workplace emergencies.

The Company conducts initial and periodic workplace inspections at all sites and offices place to identify the new or recurring hazards. This involves a thorough analysis of various factors, including tasks, responsibilities, potential risks and operational requirements.

The Company has adopted comprehensive approach to ensure a safe and healthy workplace, in line with Health, Safety, and Environment (HSE) policy of the Company. It is committed to providing a safe and healthy work environment with a specific focus on preventing injury and ill health among all employees. Each project undergoes thorough due diligence concerning quality, health, safety, and environmental standards. To minimize risks, hazards are identified and addressed proactively, while compliance with all applicable health and safety regulations is strictly maintained. Further, Regular audits, reviews, and incident investigations are conducted ensure continual improvement of the HSE management system. Link for the HSE policy:  https://kpenergy.in/kpedata/assets/uploads/Health-Safety-Environment%20Policy.pdf

The internal team of Company conducts regular audits, reviews and Safety-related accidents are thoroughly investigated. The findings from these investigations are used to prevent the recurrence of similar incidents. The effectiveness of corrective actions is evaluated during safety audits conducted. Measures are taken by the company like Health and Training sessions, first aid trainings, mock drills to ensure preparedness in handling workplace emergencies.

Note to point no. 3 of Essential Indicators : The Company ensures that all its premises and offices are fully compliant with the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016. The Company continues to promote equal employment opportunities and strives to create a work environment that respects and supports individuals with disabilities. Further, Company ensures that employees with disabilities have access to the necessary tools and resources that facilitate their work and enable them to perform their tasks effectively. These facilities include wheelchairs and ramps for the mobility-impaired, accessible restrooms. KPEL is dedicated to continuously improving our facilities to foster a welcoming and accessible environment for all individuals.

Note to point no. 4 of Essential Indicators : The Company is committed to promote an inclusive workplace that respects and supports the rights of all individuals, including persons with disabilities and strongly stands against discrimination based on gender, caste, religion, disability, or sex.

The Company identifies its key stakeholder groups through ongoing stakeholder engagement process, which includes customers, suppliers, employees, communities, government regulators and shareholders. This process allows the Company to recognise and respond stakeholders effectively. Engagement is based on regular transparent communication and a commitment to creating shared value. To support this, the Company has a Stakeholder Relationship Committee responsible for monitoring stakeholder engagement, addressing grievances, particularly those of shareholders and investors and ensuring transparent and timely communication.

The Company ensures consultation between stakeholders and the Board on economic, environmental, and social topics through structured engagement mechanisms such as stakeholder meetings, surveys, community forums, and grievance redressal systems. While day-to-day consultations are managed by relevant functional teams and senior management, insights gathered are consolidated and reported to the Board through periodic updates, dashboards and presentations by senior management. Dedicated Board-level committees such as the Stakeholder Relationship Committee, CSR Committee, and Risk Management Committee play a key role in reviewing this feedback and advising the Board/ Management on key stakeholder-related matters, ensuring stakeholder inputs are embedded into strategic planning and decision-making processes.

Stakeholder consultations are actively used to identify and manage key environmental and social topics. Through ongoing engagement using various communication channels, the Company gathers feedback on stakeholder needs, expectations, and concerns. These insights are reviewed by relevant functional teams and senior management and are incorporated into planning and strategy Cycles, CSR initiatives and compliance practices.

The Company is committed to building constructive relationships with all its stakeholders including vulnerable /marginalised groups. Engagements with stakeholders are done on diverse issues. Proactive engagement with stakeholders provides the Company with the insights that help to gain information on material issues, shape business strategy and operations and minimise the risk of reputation.

The Company has internal grievance redressal mechanism handled by Human Resources Department and the Internal Compliance Team to address human rights-related concerns. These mechanisms ensure confidentiality, fair investigation and timely resolution of complaints raised by employees or stakeholders.

KPEL is dedicated to fostering a diverse, equitable and inclusive workplace. It promotes equal opportunity for all individuals irrespective of gender, age, race, religion, caste, community, physical ability or gender identity through a strong, non-discriminatory policy framework. By creating a safe, respectful and enabling work environment, the Company empowers employees to perform at their best.
Further, KPEL is committed to maintaining a safe, inclusive and harassment-free workplace through a robust Prevention of Sexual Harassment (POSH) Policy, applicable to all group companies. A duly constituted Internal Complaints Committee (ICC), functions in line with the POSH Act, who addresses complaints confidentially and fairly. The policy outlines a clear redressal and enquiry process, ensuring non-retaliation and protection of complainants. Disciplinary action is taken against proven cases, including termination if necessary.

The Renewable energy generation and project development business does not involve any process that result in liquid discharge. Consequently, there is no impact on water resources or the environment in this regard and the company is not subject to regulations or permits related to liquid waste management.

As KPEL operates in the renewable energy sector, there is no discharge of water from its operations. The water used for washing solar panels either evaporates or is naturally absorbed into the ground. Furthermore, with the adoption of robotic module cleaning technology, KPEL has significantly reduced water consumption for solar panel maintenance. Apart from this, water is used only for domestic purposes at the project sites.

As a renewable energy company, KPEL’s core operations are inherently aligned with efforts to reduce Greenhouse Gas (GHG) emissions. By generating clean energy through solar and other renewable sources, KPEL plays a direct role in displacing conventional fossil fuel-based power generation, thereby contributing meaningfully to climate change mitigation.

In the FY2025, KPEL’s renewable energy projects have resulted in an estimated carbon dioxide (CO2) savings of 5,60,156 tons. This reflects the positive environmental impact of our clean energy generation and reinforces our commitment to a low-carbon future.

KPEL is committed to responsible environmental management across all its operations. Our waste management practices are designed to minimize environmental impact, ensure regulatory compliance and promote resource efficiency. Waste is segregated at source, with non-recyclable and hazardous waste, if any, disposed of through authorized vendors in compliance with regulations. Although the use of hazardous or toxic chemicals in our processes is minimal, preference is given to safer alternatives wherever feasible, and employees are regularly trained and made aware of appropriate handling and safety measures.
